items.title: Farioli windt zich op: 'Dit mag niet worden toegestaan ​​in een UEFA-competitie'
items.summary: Voor Francesco Farioli was het een frustrerende donderdagavond. Niet alleen kwam hij met zijn FC Porto niet langs Viktoria Plzen in de Europa League. Ook stoorde hij zich aan de beperkte blessuretijd en een gebrek aan ballen rondom het veld.

items.title: Rondje Europa: Feyenoord wint weer eens, afscheid Deijl met nederlaag
items.summary: Feyenoord maakte donderdagavond een einde aan de reeks zonder overwinningen door in de Europa League van Sturm Graz te winnen. Ook Go Ahead Eagles mag nog een sprankje hoop houden, voor FC Utrecht zit het avontuur erop. We blikken terug in Rondje Europa.

items.title: Transfer window latest, Emery shrugs off Tielemans row, and more: football – live
items.summary: ⚽ The latest football news heading into the weekend ⚽ Premier League: 10 things to look out for | Mail Niall A few Premier League managers spoke to the media on Thursday, including the man with football’s most exceptional voice, Daniel Farke. He takes his Leeds side to Everton on Monday night, and isn’t planning to make any big January signings beyond the loan move for Brighton’s Facundo Buonanotte. “We want to keep the group tight. We want to keep our togetherness and our unity. If we got the feeling there was an option that makes us better, then we would do it. But you also know my attitude - I think the January market is a difficult one, and for that, we are not in panic mode, not under any pressure to do something.” Continue reading...

items.title: 'Mats Deijl kan een publiekslieveling worden bij Feyenoord'
items.summary: Mats Deijl maakt zo goed als zeker de overstap van Go Ahead Eagles naar Feyenoord. De rechtsback speelde donderdagavond zijn laatste wedstrijd voor de Deventenaren. Is de 28-jarige Deijl een versterking voor de Rotterdammers? Go Ahead Eagles-watcher Sander Janssen bespreekt de transfer in VI ZSM.

items.title: Ze vallen bij bosjes bij Feyenoord-opponent Betis: 'Na één minuut was het pats'
items.summary: Real Betis is, een week voor het duel met Feyenoord, niet ongeschonden uit de Europese week gekomen. De Spaanse subtopper ging in de Europa League onderuit bij PAOK Saloniki (2-0). De frustratie bij trainer Manuel Pellegrini zat hem vooral in de fysieke malheur die zijn ploeg opliep.

items.title: 'Goedkoop' grapje Infantino over Britse fans leidt tot vileine reactie
items.summary: Een grapje van Gianni Infantino over het gedrag van Britse supporters tijdens het afgelopen WK in Qatar, heeft in Engeland geleid tot een vileine reactie van de supportersassociatie. Die wees de FIFA-voorzitter er fijntjes op dat hij zich misschien beter kan richten op het betaalbaar maken van de tickets voor het WK van komende zomer.

items.title: We bought a soccer team! What NBA greats Kerr, Nash & Co. learned owning LaLiga's Mallorca
items.summary: Steve Kerr and Steve Nash own Mallorca with a former USMNT international and an ex-tennis pro. What has it taught them about sport, leadership and life?

items.title: Casemiro to leave Man United at end of the season
items.summary: Casemiro will leave Manchester United when his contract expires at the end of the season, the club have confirmed.

items.title: Dual-nationals help Mexico snap winless streak
items.summary: Dual-nationals Richard Ledezma and Brian Gutierrez earned starts on Thursday as Mexico claimed a victory over Panama.

items.title: Rodman contract saga ends with record Spirit deal
items.summary: United States forward Trinity Rodman signed a record three-year contract with the Washington Spirit on Thursday to remain at the club, ending months-long uncertainty over the star's future.

items.title: Dzeko, 39, returns to Germany with eye on WC
items.summary: Edin Dzeko has returned to Germany where he hopes to play more to help Bosnia and Herzegovina earn a World Cup ticket.

items.title: Poker player’s punt on Wednesday shrouded in secrecy after Blades missteps
items.summary: James Bord’s consortium is the preferred bidder to take over Sheffield Wednesday but his data-led player recruitment record is mixed, especially with United Sheffield Wednesday fans will be delighted to hear that one associate of James Bord describes the preferred bidder for their club as “a mini Tony Bloom”, although the professional poker player’s references from the other side of the Steel City are rather less complimentary. Until it became clear late last year that Bord was planning to buy Wednesday his data company, Short Circuit Science, had a consultancy contract with Sheffield United to assist with their recruitment, which, as their position in the lower reaches of the Championship indicates, has delivered limited success. Continue reading...

items.summary: Fulham, Chelsea, Aston Villa, Nottingham Forest and Liverpool could be making moves before the window closes By WhoScored Shock: Chelsea have been linked with another young player. This time it is the Rennes centre-back Jérémy Jacquet, who would offer something the team is lacking. Continue reading...

items.title: 'None were arrested' - Infantino makes jibe at British football fans
items.summary: FIFA president Gianni Infantino made a jibe at British football fans while defending the upcoming World Cup and its ticket prices.

items.summary: We have a busy day’s racing on Friday with action from Doncaster, Southwell, Wolverhampton and Meydan, live on Sky Sports Racing...

items.title: 'He's my son' - Emery on pushing Tielemans
items.summary: Aston Villa manager Unai Emery plays down a heated touchline incident with midfielder Youri Tielemans after substituting him against Fenerbahce.

items.title: Alcaraz dazzles in trick-shot fest as Medvedev and Tien meet again
items.summary: Rod Laver Arena was treated to a festival of trick shots as Carlos Alcaraz continued his smooth Australian Open progress with victory over Corentin Moutet.

items.summary: BBC Sport Scotland examines Rangers' turn in fortunes recently under Danny Rohl.

items.summary: Martin O'Neill praised his team for claiming a draw in Italy, but big tests are still to come.

items.title: Premier League: 10 things to look out for this weekend
items.summary: Mateus Fernandes steels West Ham, Dominic Calvert-Lewin faces a homecoming and Manchester City need Marc Guéhi Top scorers: check out the latest standings The absence of the wantaway Lucas Paquetá has given Mateus Fernandes a chance to take on more responsibility for West Ham. Paquetá, who is said to be nursing a minor back problem, was unavailable again for last week’s win at Spurs but Nuno Espírito Santo’s struggling side coped without the Flamengo target. They called on Fernandes to dictate the flow in midfield and the diligent Portuguese did not disappoint. Fernandes moved the ball cleverly, picked up an assist and looked like that rarest of things: a smart signing from West Ham. They will need the 21-year-old, who joined from Southampton for £38m last summer, to shine again with Paquetá looking unlikely to return against high-flying Sunderland at the London Stadium. Jacob Steinberg West Ham v Sunderland, Saturday 12.30pm (all times GMT) Burnley v Tottenham, Saturday 3pm Fulham v Brighton, Saturday 3pm Manchester City v Wolves, Saturday 3pm Bournemouth v Liverpool, Saturday 5.30pm Continue reading...
